query: Magnificent 7 tech companies (Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, Tesla) large acquisitions $10 billion or more announced or rumored in 2026, especially mid-2026. Any pending big M&A deals as of June-July 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of my findings on Magnificent 7 large acquisitions ($10B+) in 2026:

## Bottom Line
As of mid-July 2026, there is **no newly announced or pending $10B+ Mag 7 acquisition that surfaced specifically in June–July 2026**. The two $10B+ deals of 2026 were both announced/structured in 2025:

**Confirmed $10B+ deals:**
1. **Alphabet–Wiz ($32B)** — Closed March 11, 2026; largest deal in Google's history. Announced 2025, closed Q1 2026.
2. **Nvidia–Groq (~$20B)** — Announced Dec 24, 2025; structured as a non-exclusive licensing/acqui-hire deal to avoid antitrust review. Faced ongoing Senate/FTC scrutiny into mid-2026.

**The key rumored mega-deal:**
- **Apple–Perplexity (~$14B) or Mistral** — Long-rumored (since mid-2025), still unconsummated. Some reports now say the Apple-Perplexity deal is "dead," with Apple favoring a Google Gemini partnership for Siri instead. Would be Apple's largest-ever acquisition if it happened.

**Sub-$10B / not acquisitions:**
- Apple's actual deals are small (Q.ai ~$2B, SigScalr); its $30B Broadcom deal is a supply contract, not M&A.
- Meta's $14.3B Scale AI was a 49% stake announced in June **2025**.
- Amazon, Microsoft, and Tesla have **no** announced $10B+ acquisitions in 2026.

**Context:** ~$2.3 trillion was wiped off Mag 7 value in June 2026 amid AI-spending scrutiny, and capital is flowing into infrastructure capex (~$725B combined) rather than mega-M&A. The biggest pending megadeals of 2026 (Netflix–WBD $83B, EA $55B, Charter–Cox $34.5B) are all **non-Mag-7**.

The full structured findings, data points, and sources have been recorded.

key_facts: Alphabet closed its $32 billion all-cash acquisition of cloud-security firm Wiz on March 11, 2026 — the largest deal in Alphabet's history and largest exit in Israeli tech history (announced 2025, closed Q1 2026).

Nvidia's ~$20 billion Groq deal (announced Dec 24, 2025) was structured as a non-exclusive licensing/acqui-hire agreement to avoid antitrust review; it faced a Senate inquiry (Warren/Blumenthal, March 20, 2026) and continued FTC scrutiny into mid-2026.
No new $10B+ Mag 7 acquisition was announced specifically in June–July 2026.

Apple's rumored acquisition of Perplexity AI (~$14B) or Mistral remains an active but unconsummated rumor as of mid-2026; some reports call the Apple-Perplexity deal 'dead' with Apple opting for a Google Gemini partnership for Siri instead.

Apple's actual 2026 acquisitions are small: Q.ai (~$2B, Jan 29, 2026, its second-largest ever after Beats) and SigScalr assets (July 2026). Its $30B Broadcom deal (July 8, 2026) is a chip supply contract, not an acquisition.

Meta's $14.3B Scale AI deal (49% non-voting stake, ~$29B implied valuation) was announced June 2025, not a 2026 acquisition; Meta's 2026 deals (e.g., Assured Robot Intelligence, May 2026) are small/undisclosed.
The largest pending megadeals in 2026 are non-Mag-7: Netflix–Warner Bros Discovery ($83B), Electronic Arts take-private ($55B, PIF/Silver Lake), Charter–Cox ($34.5B), BlackRock(GIP)/EQT–AES ($33.4B).
Around $2.3 trillion was wiped off Magnificent 7 market value in June 2026 amid AI-spending scrutiny (the 'air pocket'), dampening appetite for new mega-M&A.
Amazon, Microsoft, and Tesla have no announced $10B+ acquisitions in 2026; capital is directed to AI infrastructure capex (~$725B combined hyperscaler guidance for 2026).
2026 is a record M&A year overall: Q1 global deal value exceeded $1.2 trillion (LSEG), with AI-race companies among the biggest deals (Bain Midyear report, June 29, 2026).

data_points.metric: Meta Scale AI investment (49% non-voting stake)
Mag 7 market value lost in June 2026 (AI-spending air pocket)
Netflix pending acquisition of Warner Bros Discovery (largest pending non-Mag-7 deal)
Combined hyperscaler AI capex guidance for 2026
